title: Advanced Ovarian Cancer
chief_complaint: My belly has been getting bigger for 3 months. I feel full quickly, I've lost 15 pounds, and I have pelvic pain.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Distended, shifting dullness, fluid wave. Nodular pelvic mass palpated.
physical_exam.Pelvic: Fixed, irregular pelvic mass. Rectovaginal nodularity.

hidden_data.ct_abdomen: Bilateral adnexal masses with solid/cystic components. Omental caking. Peritoneal carcinomatosis. Massive ascites. Liver metastases.
hidden_data.biopsy: Omental biopsy: High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma. BRCA1 mutation positive.

correct_diagnosis: Stage IVB High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma (BRCA1 Positive)
key_learning_points: Ovarian cancer = leading cause of gynecologic cancer death. 75% present at Stage III-IV because symptoms are vague and late
Risk: BRCA1 (40% lifetime risk), BRCA2 (20%), Lynch syndrome, Nulliparity. Protective: OCPs (50% reduction with >5 years use), Tubal ligation, Salpingectomy
Symptoms: Bloating, early satiety, pelvic pain, urinary urgency, fatigue. Symptom index: >12 days/month for <1 year → requires evaluation

Treatment: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy (Carboplatin+Paclitaxel) → Interval debulking → Maintenance PARP inhibitor (Olaparib) if BRCA+. Optimal cytoreduction (<1cm residual) by GYN oncologist improves survival
Prognosis: Stage I 90%, Stage III 40-50%, Stage IV 20-30%. BRCA+ has better prognosis (more platinum-sensitive). Genetic testing for ALL ovarian cancer patients (NCCN)

feedback.attending_note: GYN oncology referral. Neoadjuvant Carboplatin AUC 5 + Paclitaxel 175mg/m² q3w ×3 cycles. Interval debulking surgery: TAH+BSO+Omentectomy+Lymphadenectomy. Optimal cytoreduction goal <1cm. Post-op chemo ×3 cycles. Maintenance Olaparib 300mg BID (BRCA+). Genetic counseling for family. Paracentesis for symptomatic ascites.
